scenario: An Inclusive Gateway has 3 outgoing flows; their condition expressions are =score > 80, =age >= 65, =isVip = true. The modeler wants to also designate a default flow to take when none of the conditions match.
question: Which configuration sets the default flow?

options.text: Add a condition expression =true on the designated default sequence flow. A FEEL expression that always evaluates to true is not a default flow -- it would be taken on every execution regardless of whether other conditions match, potentially activating this path alongside other matching branches.

Add a fourth sequence flow without any condition expression and expect Zeebe to auto-detect it as the default. Zeebe does not auto-detect default flows. An unconditioned outgoing flow from a gateway where other flows have conditions would cause an error or unpredictable routing at runtime. The default flow must be explicitly designated using the BPMN default attribute pointing to a specific sequence flow ID.

Set the default attribute on the Inclusive Gateway, referencing the ID of one outgoing sequence flow. That designated flow has no condition expression and is taken only when none of the other outgoing conditions (=score > 80, =age >= 65, =isVip = true) evaluate to true. This is the BPMN-standard mechanism for defining a fallback path on both Exclusive and Inclusive gateways.

Default flows apply only to Exclusive Gateways and are unavailable on Inclusive Gateways. This is factually flawed. 0 specification allows the default attribute on both Exclusive and Inclusive Gateways. Camunda 8 Zeebe honours the default attribute on Inclusive Gateway outgoing sequence flows, enabling a no-match fallback path to be designated explicitly when none of the other conditional paths evaluate to true at runtime.

a.text: Incorrect. BPMN gateways support a default attribute referencing one outgoing sequence flow. That flow has no condition and is the fallback when no other condition matches. Works on both Exclusive and Inclusive gateways.

b.text: Incorrect. =true always evaluates true -- would take that flow regardless of others.

d.text: Incorrect. Inclusive also supports default flows.
